esST_projectObjective:
The overall objective of the project (both Modules) is to is to satisfy needs expressed by Commission and to provide monitoring indicators defined in Digital Decade Policy Programme by all countries included in the European Statistical System. The digitalization goals are set for the for pillars of the Programme: `
•	Skills 
•	Digital transformation of enterprises 
•	A secure and sustainable digital infrastructure 
•	Digitization of public service
The surveys on ICT Usage in enterprises and households are main data  sources for five of the key performance indicators (KPIs) of this Programme Artificial Intelligence, cloud computing, big data (data analytics), the digital intensity index (DII) - a composite indicator reflecting the digital transformation of business, and the digital skills indicator (DSI) - a composite indicator measuring the digital skills of citizens based on the Digital Competence Framework (DigComp 2.0).

The short-term effect of the project (both modules) will be to measure the current level of digitization of European economies and societies. In the long-term perspective, having indicators from several years, it will be possible to create analyses applying long time series or analyses with large time retrospection. The target groups are primarily the research community and policy makers. The latter will use indicators relevant for Digital Decade Policy Programme to monitor national and EU-level trajectories. Additional microdata linking will allow to analyse impact of ICT usage on productivity of enterprises.
title: Digitalisation grants

esST_projectObjective:
"This project is aimed at fulfilling Commission Regulation implementing Regulation (EU) 2019/2152 of the European Parliament and Council of 17 December 2019, laying down the technical specifications of data requirements for the topic ""ICT usage and e-commerce"" for the survey year 2026. In addition, it also covers the implementation of Regulation (EU) 2019/1700 for the survey on the use of ICT in households and by individuals for 2026. The overall objective of this grant is to ensure the provision of high quality and comparable data on ICT statistics.
This project shall cover 14 optional variables from Module 1.1 in the ICT enterprises survey and 24 optional variables from Sub-module 2.3 in the ICT households survey."
title: Digitalisation Grants
esIN_objective:

esST_legalEntityNames: INSTITUT NATIONAL DE LA STATISTIQUEDES ETUDES ECONOMIQUES
esST_projectObjective:
Module 2: STATEC plan to conduct an annual sample survey of the resident population of Luxembourg, aged 16-74, with the aim of collecting detailed micro-data on the use of Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) by households and individuals. This survey is carried out every year by STATEC Luxembourg in accordance with the requirements of the European Framework Regulation on Social Statistics (IESS). 
In addition to the micro-data, a metadata report is produced and sent to Eurostat, providing information on the design of the survey and the statistical reliability of the data collected and the indicators derived. 
Statistical indicators will be published on the STATEC web portal and in ad hoc publications.
title: Digitalisation grants

esIN_objective:
Module 2: STATEC plan to conduct an annual sample survey of the resident population of Luxembourg, aged 16-74, with the aim of collecting detailed micro-data on the use of Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) by households and individuals. This survey is carried out every year by STATEC Luxembourg in accordance with the requirements of the European Framework Regulation on Social Statistics (IESS). 
In addition to the micro-data, a metadata report is produced and sent to Eurostat, providing information on the design of the survey and the statistical reliability of the data collected and the indicators derived. 
Statistical indicators will be published on the STATEC web portal and in ad hoc publications.

esST_projectObjective: The general objective of the project is to ensure the provision of high quality and comparable data for the production of statistics on the usage of Information and Communication Technologies for survey year 2026 in enterprises and in households and by individuals, in line with the legal framework and specific instructions given by Eurostat, as well as to provide additional information for measuring digitalisation which is not covered by the mandatory parts of the ICT surveys.
title: Digitalisation grants
esIN_objective: The general objective of the project is to ensure the provision of high quality and comparable data for the production of statistics on the usage of Information and Communication Technologies for survey year 2026 in enterprises and in households and by individuals, in line with the legal framework and specific instructions given by Eurostat, as well as to provide additional information for measuring digitalisation which is not covered by the mandatory parts of the ICT surveys.

esST_projectObjective: Since 2005, HCSO has successfully applied for grants Statistics on the usage of information and communication technologies. Based on the reliable professional experience, HCSO would like to submit the proposal for Digitalisation grants, ‘Statistics on the usage of ICT in enterprises’ (module 1) and ‘Statistics on the use of ICT in households and by individuals’ (module 2). Module 1: Hungary’s main objective is to provide accurate and reliable data in addition to the mandatory indictors for the optional variables for all required NACE Rev.2.1 activity and NUTS 2 breakdown categories fulfilling the enhanced coverage for the statistics on ICT usage and e-commerce in enterprises for the survey year 2026 and to better understand the impact of digitalisation on productivity. These objectives are fully compliant to the regulation (EU) No 2019/2152 on European business statistics and its implementing Regulation specifying the data requirements and their coverage, as well as the deadlines for submission of metadata and quality reports. As a result of the survey, Hungarian Central Statistical Office (hereinafter HCSO) and Eurostat will have internationally comparable, harmonized data on ICT usage and e-commerce in enterprises. Module 2: According to the Regulation (EU)2019/1700 of the European Parliament and of the Council from samples, and its implementing and delegated Regulations, Hungary is required to provide data on household access to ICT devices and ICT use by individuals. Hungary intends to carry out its survey on the use of ICT in households and by individuals for 2026. Our objective is to collect and transmit all mandatory and optional socio-demographic information, regional of residence data on NUTS2 level, to provide all the optional variables in the topic information society participation and to extend the data collection on the age group 75-89. HCSO also intends to prepare and transmit in time the quality report and the metadata to Eurostat via the SIMS system.
title: Digitalisation grants

These objectives are fully compliant to the regulation (EU) No 2019/2152 on European business statistics and its implementing Regulation specifying the data requirements and their coverage, as well as the deadlines for submission of metadata and quality reports. As a result of the survey, Hungarian Central Statistical Office (hereinafter HCSO) and Eurostat will have internationally comparable, harmonized data on ICT usage and e-commerce in enterprises. Module 2: According to the Regulation (EU)2019/1700 of the European Parliament and of the Council from samples, and its implementing and delegated Regulations, Hungary is required to provide data on household access to ICT devices and ICT use by individuals. Hungary intends to carry out its survey on the use of ICT in households and by individuals for 2026. Our objective is to collect and transmit all mandatory and optional socio-demographic information, regional of residence data on NUTS2 level, to provide all the optional variables in the topic information society participation and to extend the data collection on the age group 75-89. HCSO also intends to prepare and transmit in time the quality report and the metadata to Eurostat via the SIMS system.

esST_projectObjective: With digitalisation grants Statistics Finland will collect and deliver additional optional data on the usage of Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) in enterprises and in households and by individuals. Most data on the use of ICT is collected on mandatory basis. To satisfy additional needs of information about the use of ICT as expressed by the Commission services, additional data as specified in this project will be collected. In addition to the use of ICT also data for measuring impact of digitalisation on productivity will be compiled and transmitted to Eurostat. The overall purpose of the project is to ensure the provision of high quality and comparable data for the production of ICT statistics.
title: Digitalisation grants
